Such a fun place!  Great combination of historic and nostalgic.  Fascinating to see early mechanical amusement machines, many handmade, some even made from toothpicks by prisoners. Learned about the origins of the industry, many early machines were brief mechanical shows. Some were brief picture shows, some risqué (by early 1900’s standards). It’s wonderful that you can play almost everything there for just a few quarters.  Several money changing machines on hand to make it easy to convert your dollars to quarters
so you can keep playing. 
For the nostalgic part it was fun to reconnect with pin ball machines and video games of our youth; we even got our photos taken in the Photo Booth. 
The proprietor is easy to spot as he is on roller skates.  I also enjoyed seeing people of all ages enjoying themselves playing games and cheering each other on; would make a great inter generational activity.

One of the best retro museums to pass the time. It's free to enter but the machines take quarters. Pinball machines and old retro arcade machines galore. Definitely worth a visit if you're in Fisherman's Wharf.

What an odd, interesting, “museum of the weird” kind of place.  We were early for our sunset cruise, so we swung into this place to kill some time.  We were not disappointed.  It’s a carnival/video game collectors dream - and possibly a nightmare.  Some of the older items made you wonder how kids from earlier generations slept at night after going to a local carnival.  But to see the games and attractions was really cool.  The engineer in me loved it. Each items works (or at least all the ones we put a quarter in did).  To the guys/gals who maintain these and show them off - kudos to you.  It was truly a gem to have found this place.
